mission: Use the primary isolated-horizon puncture constraints to determine whether the standard puncture-U(1) sector automatically produces a nonzero index degree, or whether an additional lift theorem is required.

theorem_1_external_puncture_neutrality.statement: In the standard isolated-horizon U(1) puncture quantization, puncture labels are discrete classes a_i in Z_k constrained by global gauge invariance to satisfy a neutral sum condition Σ_i a_i = 0 mod k. Therefore the standard puncture data are globally neutral at the level of the horizon Chern-Simons labels.

theorem_2_no_naive_index_from_neutral_la.statement: Because the standard puncture labels are only Z_k-valued and satisfy global neutrality, they do not by themselves determine a nonzero integral Chern degree on the closed sphere. A naive total-charge reading does not produce the nonzero integer n_punct required by the Paper 35 index route.

theorem_3_exact_remaining_lift.status: derived / strongest exact current frontier reduction
theorem_3_exact_remaining_lift.statement: The exact remaining positive route is an integral lift theorem from neutral puncture Chern-Simons data to a nonzero spin^c index degree seen by the fermionic bridge. Equivalently, one needs a theorem
theorem_3_exact_remaining_lift.exact_missing_map: {a_i in Z_k with Σ_i a_i = 0 mod k} -> n_punct in Z \ {0}
theorem_3_exact_remaining_lift.meaning: Without such a lift, the standard puncture-U(1) sector remains globally neutral and does not revive Q5.

final_boundary.status: derived / strongest current Paper 35 lift-obstruction boundary
final_boundary.safe_statement: The standard isolated-horizon puncture-U(1) sector does not automatically rescue Paper 35 baryogenesis. Its labels are globally neutral. The only live route is a new theorem lifting those neutral Z_k puncture labels to a nonzero integral spin^c degree. That lift is not present on the current stack.
